key_points: The story shows the relationship between humans and nature through Rakesh’s care for the cherry tree
The cherry tree symbolizes patience, perseverance, and the beauty of slow growth
Despite facing several obstacles, the tree continues to grow, showing the resilience of nature
Rakesh develops a deep emotional connection with the tree because he planted and nurtured it
The story highlights how small beginnings can grow into meaningful achievements
examples: The goat eating the leaves represents obstacles in life
The tree being cut by the scythe represents unexpected setbacks
The tree growing again shows the resilience of nature
Birds visiting the tree show its role in the natural ecosystem
Rakesh feeling proud of the tree reflects the satisfaction of nurturing life
formulas_or_processes: Initiation → Rakesh plants the cherry seed
Challenge → The tree faces natural and accidental damage
Resilience → The tree continues to grow despite difficulties
Achievement → The tree blossoms and produces cherries
Reflection → Rakesh realizes the joy of nurturing life
exam_tips: Explain the symbolism of the cherry tree in the story
Discuss how the story represents patience and perseverance
Understand how the author uses nature imagery
Be able to explain the deeper meaning of Rakesh’s pride in the tree
